message:
fix: handle ChatGPT parts content in flatten_content (bd-1bh.44)

flatten_content() didn't handle ChatGPT's {"content_type": "text",
"parts": [...]} content format. When a ChatGPT-sourced session was
written to Gemini format, the Gemini reader's flatten_content call
returned empty strings for all messages, causing read-back verification
to fail with "wrote 4 messages, read back 0".

Added parts array handling in the Object match arm. This also removes
the gpt->gmi known-failure workaround from the e2e matrix test — all
182 conversion pairs now pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1019,18 +1019,6 @@ assert_file_count "enrich dry-run writes no files" "$CODEX_HOME/sessions" 0
 
 ALL_ALIASES=(cc cod gmi cur cln aid amp opc gpt cwb vib fac ocl pi)
 
-# Known failing pairs (pipeline bugs tracked separately).
-# Format: "source->target"
-MATRIX_KNOWN_FAILURES="gpt->gmi"
-
-is_known_failure() {
-    local pair="$1"
-    case " $MATRIX_KNOWN_FAILURES " in
-        *" $pair "*) return 0 ;;
-        *) return 1 ;;
-    esac
-}
-
 # Set up a source session and echo its session ID.
 # CC/Codex/Gemini use native fixtures; others are seeded from CC.
 setup_source_session() {
@@ -1072,11 +1060,6 @@ for source in "${ALL_ALIASES[@]}"; do
         MATRIX_PAIRS=$((MATRIX_PAIRS + 1))
         local_pair="${source}->${target}"
 
-        if is_known_failure "$local_pair"; then
-            skip "matrix:${local_pair} (known pipeline bug)"
-            continue
-        fi
-
         run_casr "matrix:${local_pair}" --json resume "$target" "$source_sid" --source "$source"
 
         if [[ "$LAST_EXIT" -eq 0 ]]; then
